title: Federal Funds Rate History 1990 to 2026
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The federal funds rate was high in the early 1990s, dropped significantly in the 2000s, and has fluctuated since then, including a recent range of 3.5%-3.75% in
excerpt: The federal funds rate was high in the early 1990s, dropped significantly in the 2000s, and has fluctuated since then, including a recent range of 3.5%-3.75% in 2026. For detailed historical data, consult the St. Louis Fed's FRED database.

content.introduction: The federal funds rate was high in the early 1990s, dropped significantly in the 2000s, and has fluctuated since then, including a recent range of 3.5%-3.75% in 2026. For detailed historical data, consult the St. Louis Fed's FRED database.

[Farran Powell](https://www.forbes.com/advisor/author/fpowell/)Staff Editor. ![Image 29: Federal Funds Rate History 1990 to 2026](https://thumbor.forbes.com/thumbor/fit-in/900x510/https://www.forbes.com/advisor/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/history_of_fed_decisions.jpeg.jpg)Getty. ​​”Policymakers know all too well that when real rates are negative for an extended period of time, inflation pressures rise swiftly and dramatically,” Rich Yamarone, the director of economic research at Argus Research at the time, told [CNNMoney](https://money.cnn.com/2008/04/30/news/economy/fed_decision/index.htm?postversion=2008043016). *   [What Is Investing?](https://www.forbes.com/advisor/investing/what-is-investing/ "What Is Investing?"). *   [What Is A Bond?](https://www.forbes.com/advisor/investing/what-is-a-bond/ "What Is A Bond?"). *   [What Is Inflation & How Does It Work?](https://www.forbes.com/advisor/investing/what-is-inflation/ "What Is Inflation & How Does It Work?"). *   [What Is a Recession?](https://www.forbes.com/advisor/investing/what-is-a-recession/ "What Is a Recession?"). The Fed has not cut rates so far in 2026. Today, the federal funds rate stands at a range of 3.5%-3.75%. “Available indicators suggest that

For additional historical federal funds rate data, please see [Daily Federal Funds Rate from 1928-1954](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/categories/33951). Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System (US), Federal Funds Effective Rate [FEDFUNDS], retrieved from FRED, Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis; https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS, May 4, 2026.
